original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy
original_registry_entry.population: People undergoing gastrointestinal endoscopy with recommendation-specific sedation, procedure, age, and comorbidity contexts.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Endoscopy pre-assessment, procedure, recovery, and discharge.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact only for selected procedure-context, monitored-recovery, discharge-assessment, follow-up-planning, and safety-instruction documentation. It does not support retrospective diagnosis, pathology interpretation, treatment, or universal post-endoscopy follow-up.

exact_sections.heading: Pre-assessment — indication, intended aim, and procedure context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the endoscopic procedure context and indication or intended aim as known clinical-record information.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 16–17 — monitoring and complete recovery
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 18–19 — suitability for discharge and instructions
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports formal documented discharge assessment, recovery from sedation, absence of complications, vital and respiratory status, pain or discomfort review, procedure-appropriate instructions, contact information, and activity restrictions after sedation.

title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy
population: People undergoing gastrointestinal endoscopy with recommendation-specific sedation, procedure, age, and comorbidity contexts.
intended_setting: Endoscopy pre-assessment, procedure, recovery, and discharge.
access_limitations: One or more PDF tables require structured extraction
Algorithm or figure references preserved as locators; diagram structure not interpreted
